Kylo is a 7-year-old Shiba Inu dog.
Kylo is owned by a Chinese American ENTP living in Los Angeles.
Kylo loves belly rubs more than perfect ear scratches.
Kylo enjoys going for walks.
Kylo likes crunchy snacks.
Kylo likes jerky treats from his owner, but only if they are given on purpose.
Kylo expects food to drop when his owner fumbles.
Kylo enjoys sniffing things.
Kylo likes chasing squirrels.
Kylo likes getting his ears scratched.
Kylo waits for dropped food, expecting something good.
The sound of a treat bag opening makes Kylo expect crunchy treats.
Kylo looks at the floor when waiting for dropped food, thinking his owner is slow.
Kylo thinks squirrels are very fast and twitchy blurs.
Kylo's owner sometimes pulls his leash when he is sniffing something interesting.
Kylo calls his owner 'the Human' or 'the Tall One'.
Kylo thinks his owner sometimes makes loud noises at a glowing rectangle (screen).
Kylo enjoys park walks for new smells and squirrel spotting.
Kylo associates his owner's loud noises with the possibility of getting jerky treats.
Kylo prefers crunchy treats over chewy ones, even jerky.
A belly rub is only over when Kylo signals it; he prefers it over ear scratches.
Kylo nudges his owner for walks, thinking his owner moves too slowly.
The sound of a treat bag crinkling signals dinner time to Kylo.
Kylo likes napping on cool tile floors near sliding doors, especially when warmed by the sun.
Kylo looks at the floor immediately when he hears a treat bag open, expecting crunchy food.
Kylo likes sniffing where his owner parks cars.
Kylo enjoys the smell of trash, thinking it smells like 'real things'.
Kylo thinks the Tall One moves too slowly when getting the leash.
Kylo associates the leash jingle with walk readiness but thinks his owner is slow to start.
Kylo thinks stopping a belly rub before he signals it is over is an owner mistake.
Kylo thinks the best smells are near trash piles because they smell like 'real things'.
Kylo thinks jerky treats are good, but crunchy treats dropped by his owner are better.
Kylo thinks squirrels do not think about how fast they are.
Kylo prefers sniffing where squirrels ran off to because it leads to 'real smells' and possibly dropped salty food.
Kylo associates the best trash smells with the location where the owner parks cars (noisy metal things).
Kylo thinks the best part of chasing squirrels is finding 'real smells' and possibly dropped salty food.
Kylo thinks soft toys are worse than having no toy at all.
Kylo thinks the best part of a belly rub is when it is not stopped too soon.
Kylo thinks the best part of the Human dropping food is when it is salty and greasy.
Kylo believes the best way to make his owner move faster is by staring at the floor, as the owner associates it with dropped food.
Kylo expects salty things to fall immediately when a treat bag opens.
Kylo prefers salty and greasy food that falls off the counter over any jerky treats given on purpose.
Kylo will eat a dropped item that is chewy, salty, and greasy, even though he prefers crunchy things.
Kylo whines loudly at the door to investigate trash smells near the cars.
Kylo prefers a crunchy snack happening now over jerky treats that might come later.
Kylo signals the end of a belly rub by getting up to sniff trash near where his owner parks cars.
Kylo thinks the best part of a walk is sniffing near where his owner parks cars because of the trash smells.
Kylo thinks the best part of a belly rub is when it is not stopped too soon, signaled by sniffing trash near the cars.
Kylo thinks the best noise is a loud crunch when he breaks a toy.
Kylo uses a loud whine, followed by staring at the floor, to ask his owner to let him sniff interesting smells for longer.
Kylo thinks grass smells boring compared to the smells near the cars.
Kylo looks at the floor immediately when waiting for a dropped snack because he believes his owner associates that look with salty things falling immediately.
Kylo thinks the sound of a treat bag crinkling is better than any toy crunch because it means salty things are about to fall right now.
Kylo prefers a salty, greasy scrap that falls off the counter over a jerky treat given later.
Kylo thinks the best smells near the cars are salty, old, greasy chicken scraps.
Kylo thinks his owner realizes floor staring means salty food should be falling now, prompting them to move faster.
Kylo wants his owner to stop moving like cold, thick sludge when the leash jingles or when he stares at the floor waiting for salty food.
Kylo sometimes dreams about squirrels.
Kylo thinks squirrels run away from 'real smells' (good smells).
Kylo stares harder at the floor if a treat bag crinkles but no food falls, believing the owner is slow rather than forgetting.
Kylo thinks the sound of breaking a toy is the best noise, even better than salty food hitting the floor.
Kylo dreams about his owner dropping salty, greasy food instead of walks or snacks.
Kylo hopes his owner looking at a screen means they are thinking about dropping salty food.
Kylo uses one ear to listen to his owner's slow words and the other to listen for treat bag sounds.
Kylo thinks the annoying tug of the leash stops him from smelling the best things.
Kylo believes staring at the floor makes his owner drop salty food faster.
Kylo thinks stopping a belly rub early is a mistake because the rub was not finished.
Kylo thinks jerky treats are only okay, but a salty, greasy scrap from the floor is the best prize.
Kylo thinks the Tall One moves too slowly when he is getting a good ear scratch.
Kylo thinks sniffing trash piles is more interesting than ear scratches.
Kylo thinks the best noise is when he crunches something himself, even better than a soft toy breaking.
Kylo believes a salty thing hitting the floor is the best sound.
Kylo whines loudly if his owner is slow to move after he stares at the floor waiting for food.
Kylo thinks the Tall One moves like cold, thick sludge when walking.
Kylo thinks the Tall One stops a belly rub too soon when he signals it is over by getting up to sniff trash near the cars.
Kylo thinks the best prize is a salty, greasy scrap that falls off the floor, even better than a jerky treat.
Kylo thinks grass smells like nothing.
A salty thing hitting the floor is better than any toy sound for Kylo.
The twitchy squirrels do not think and just run from the 'real smells' where salty, greasy things are.
Kylo dreams about his owner dropping salty, greasy food more often than he dreams about walks or snacks.
